description_long: This is a rectangular, mid-rise office building with a brick facade and a flat, dark roof. The structure features four stories, each lined with evenly spaced, rectangular windows that are symmetrically arranged across all visible sides. The ground floor is distinguished by a series of large, glass storefront windows or entryways, separated by brick columns, suggesting commercial or lobby spaces at street level. The upper floors have a repetitive window pattern, with each window framed by light-colored trim, contributing to a sense of order and regularity. The building's exterior is primarily composed of reddish-brown brickwork, with subtle horizontal banding and a simple cornice detail at the roofline. There is no visible signage or decorative ornamentation, giving the building a utilitarian, early 20th-century commercial architectural style.
description: A four-story, rectangular office building with a brick facade, flat roof, and regularly spaced windows. The ground floor features large glass storefronts or entryways, while the upper floors have a uniform window pattern.
description_view_0: The long facade of the building is visible, showing four stories of evenly spaced windows and a row of large glass storefronts or entryways at the ground level.
description_view_1: The short side of the building is shown, highlighting the building's depth and the same regular window arrangement on a narrower facade.
description_view_2: This view mirrors view 0, displaying the opposite long facade with identical window and storefront arrangements.
description_view_3: The opposite short side is visible, again showing the building's depth and the consistent window pattern on the narrower face.
attribute_window_pattern: Uniformly spaced, rectangular windows arranged in horizontal rows and vertical columns, with each floor having a consistent and repetitive pattern.
